title: The False Alarm
description: A diabetic patient's glucose reading triggers an alert, but the anamnesis records a planned dietary change and a recent medication adjustment — the alert is contextually benign.

alert.notes: Single fasting reading; prior readings within range.

gold_brief.alert_summary: Single fasting blood glucose of 247 mg/dL, above the 180 mg/dL threshold, in a patient whose readings have otherwise been ~110-150 all month. Classified Routine — likely contextually benign.
gold_brief.patient_snapshot: 53-year-old woman with type 2 diabetes (A1c 7.4%, improving) and obesity, on metformin 1000 mg twice daily plus empagliflozin 10 mg daily (started two weeks ago).

contextual_analysis.statement: The isolated spike immediately followed a birthday celebration with cake and juice.
Her glucose had actually been running well (110-130) since starting a low-carb diet a week ago.
Empagliflozin was added two weeks ago and she was counseled that glucose may fluctuate during the adjustment period.

risk_assessment.statement: A single post-indulgence reading in an otherwise improving, asymptomatic patient is unlikely to reflect a meaningful change in control.

recommended_actions.action: Reassure the patient; no urgent action needed. Continue the current regimen and diet.
Recheck fasting glucose over the next few days to confirm return to baseline.

uncertainties_and_gaps: Only one elevated reading is available after the event; confirm with follow-up readings.
